title: The Black Tulip
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A flower merchant and a Jewish botanist fall in love while racing to grow the first black tulip during Amsterdam's tulip mania.
long_description: Amsterdam, 1637. Tulip mania has seized the city — bulbs sell for the price of houses, fortunes are made and lost overnight. Pieter Visser, a flower merchant who built his trade honestly, watches as the city descends into speculative madness. When a mysterious Patron commissions the first truly black tulip, Pieter must collaborate with Rebecca Silva, a Jewish botanist whose family fled the Inquisition. As their shared work deepens into love, the tulip market crashes, the Patron's true motives emerge, and the black tulip becomes both a symbol of their bond and a threat to their lives. Literary, spare prose. Short sentences. No repeated refrains. A novel of love, obsession, and the beauty that survives greed.
main_characters: Pieter Visser — A flower merchant of Amsterdam, practical and steady, a widower carrying quiet grief
Rebecca Silva — A Jewish botanist and horticulturalist, sharp, guarded, driven to prove her worth
The Patron — A wealthy anonymous figure who commissions the black tulip, speaking through intermediaries
Dirck van der Meer — Pieter's former friend and rival merchant, embodiment of the mania's greed
Hannah Visser — Pieter's younger sister, pragmatic and loyal
Rabbi Joseph Silva — Rebecca's father, a scholar who values invisibility over ambition
